free_text: Experimental determination of cross section of
alpha-induced reactions on nat-Pd.
Main reference
Preliminary data.
A stack containing 14 high purity Pd foils , thickness
7.99 mum was irradiated at the VUB cyclotron with a
36.5 MeV alpha-particle beam. Interleaved high purity
Cu foils (7.61 mum thick, purity >99.6%) served as
monitor foils to obtain reliable beam characteristics
(energy and intensity) by comparison of the cross
sections of the monitor reactions with reference values
over the whole energy range studied. These foils  also
allowed checking the energy degradation in the stack.
The following individual uncertainties are
included in the propagated error-
Uncertainty in the number of target nuclei
including non-uniformity,
Uncertainty in the incident particle intensity
Uncertainty in the detection efficiency,
Uncertainty in the determination of
the peak areas including statistical errors,
Uncertainty in the absolute abundance of
the used gamma rays.
*By authors*. The strongly non-linear effect of the
possible uncertainty of the half-lives for isotopes
with small T1/2 was not taken into account.
Tbl.2 from Nucl.Instr.Meth.,B229(2005)321
F.C
New reference was added.

*By authors*.The radionuclide 104Cd is formed by
direct production through (a,2n) and (a,4n) reactions
on the stable isotopes 102Pd (1.02%) and 104Pd
respectively. In the energy domain studied only the
(a,2n) reaction seems to contribute as the excitation
function is still decreasing at the maximum energy in
the foils although it is situated slightly above the
threshold of the (a,4n) reaction.
.Zero was added after decimal points in
Data-lines.
*By authors*.The sharp rise around 30 MeV and the
levelling at the highest energy studied seems to
indicate that the 104-Pd(a,3n) reactions plays a
dominant role.

was from the 104-Cd parent. The uncertainties on a
tentative separation based on the single time
measurement of the different signals were so high that
no reliable result for direct Ag-104-g cross section.

produced through the direct reaction 110Pd(a,p+n). The
practical threshold lies here also about 8 MeV above
the theoretical limit. The reaction cross section,
obtained by taking into account the abundance of 110-Pd
in nat-Pd (11.72%) reaches a maximum in the
neighbourhood of 37-38 MeV with a maximal value 62.5 mb
